county_metadata.record_losses: No significant losses. County Clerk records preserved from colonial period. Some Dutch-era documents transferred to NY State Archives.
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: One of the original 12 counties (1683). Albany was colonial capital under Dutch and English rule. Originally encompassed most of upstate NY; many counties carved from it. County Clerk maintains land records; Surrogate's Court handles probate.
county_metadata.research_tips: Albany County is the seat of state government and home to the New York State Archives and New York State Library, making it the single most important county for NY genealogical research. The State Archives on Madison Avenue holds colonial-era Dutch records, British provincial papers, and state census records (1825-1925). The Albany County Hall of Records has deed books from 1630 and probate from 1660. The Albany Institute of History and Art has manuscripts of prominent Dutch-English families (Van Rensselaer, Schuyler, Ten Broeck). For Dutch Reformed Church records, check First Church in Albany (from 1683) published by Holland Society.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Deed records from 1630 Dutch era; online index through county clerk

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed and mortgage records from Dutch colonial period with grantor/grantee indexes.
Wills, administrations, guardianships, estate inventories from colonial era.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Complete from 1660. Early records in Dutch and English.

source_profiles.source_name: First Church Albany Dutch Reformed Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Baptisms, marriages, deaths from Albany's First Dutch Reformed Church published by Holland Society.
State census every 10 years 1825-1875, then 1892, 1905, 1915, 1925. All household members from 1855.
Marriage certificates from 1908 statewide registration. Earlier in church and town clerk records.
Muster rolls, descriptive books for Albany County regiments. Albany was a major staging area.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Excellent. Comprehensive Civil War records at State Archives.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Digitized Albany Argus, Evening Journal, Times-Union. Massive free NY newspaper archive.

county_metadata.record_losses: No significant courthouse fires. Records well preserved from formation.
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created from Genesee County in 1806. Southern Tier along PA border. Contains part of Allegany Indian Reservation (Seneca Nation). Alfred University located here. Rural agricultural county.
county_metadata.research_tips: Allegany County Clerk in Belmont holds deed records from 1806 and Surrogate's Court has probate from the same year. For pre-1806 research check Genesee County. Location on PA border means families crossed state lines frequently so also check Potter and McKean County PA records. The Allegany County Historical Society in Angelica maintains genealogical files and cemetery transcriptions. Seneca Nation records for the Allegany Reservation are held separately and not through county offices. Alfred University Herrick Library has local history. Most families arrived after 1800 from New England via Southern Tier Turnpike.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Deed records from 1806; index at county clerk

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed and mortgage records from county formation.
Wills, administrations, guardianships from 1806.
Federal census from 1810. Agricultural schedules 1850-1880.

source_profiles.source_name: Allegany County Town Clerk Vital Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Birth and death records from statewide registration 1881.

source_profiles.source_name: Baptist and Methodist Records — Allegany Co
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Church records from Baptist and Methodist congregations in the Southern Tier.
Belmont Dispatch, Wellsville Reporter, Alfred Sun digitized.

county_metadata.record_losses: No significant losses. Records transferred from New York County at formation.
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created from New York County in 1914, newest county in NY. Coterminous with Borough of the Bronx (NYC 1898). Pre-1914 records under New York County. Bronx County Courthouse built 1933.
county_metadata.research_tips: Bronx County is NYC's newest county (1914) so all pre-1914 records are filed under New York County (Manhattan). The NYC Municipal Archives at 31 Chambers Street holds birth records (1866+), death records (1795+), and marriage records (1866+) for the Bronx. Bronx County Clerk has deeds from 1914; earlier deeds at Manhattan Register Office. The Bronx County Historical Society on Bainbridge Avenue has neighborhood history files, photographs, and oral histories documenting immigrant communities (Irish, Italian, Jewish, Puerto Rican). Fordham University Archives hold Fordham Manor area records. Van Cortlandt family papers at NY Historical Society document the colonial estate.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Deed records from 1914; pre-1914 at New York County Register

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Birth certificates from 1866 (filed under New York County). Post-1909 from NYC Health Dept.
Deed and mortgage records from 1914.
Death records for Bronx area. Pre-1898 under Westchester towns.
Wills and estates from 1914. Pre-1914 at New York County Surrogate.
Pre-1874 under Westchester; 1874-1914 under New York County; 1920+ Bronx County.
Successive immigrant waves: Irish, German, Italian, Jewish, Puerto Rican. Photos, oral histories.

source_profiles.source_name: Bronx Home News and Local Papers
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Bronx Home News (1907-1948) and other local papers with obituaries and community news.

county_metadata.record_losses: No significant courthouse fires. Records intact from formation.
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created from Tioga County in 1806. Binghamton industrial center (shoes, cigars, later IBM). Southern Tier on PA border. Chenango Canal connected to Erie Canal.
county_metadata.research_tips: Broome County Clerk in Binghamton holds deed records from 1806. Surrogate's Court has probate from same year. Pre-1806 check Tioga County. Binghamton attracted immigrants from Eastern Europe — Russian Empire and Austria-Hungary. The Broome County Historical Society maintains extensive genealogical files. Broome County Public Library has local history room with city directories from 1857. For Endicott-Johnson Shoe Company worker records (employing thousands of immigrants) check the Historical Society. IBM heritage (1920s+) brought professionals documented in company town records. The Roberson Museum has manuscript collections on local industry and families.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Deed records from county formation; online index available

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed and mortgage records from 1806.
Wills, administrations, guardianships from 1806.
Federal census from 1810. Manufacturing schedules document industrial growth.
Declarations and petitions from county and federal courts. Large Eastern European immigrant pop.
Birth, death records from statewide registration.
Binghamton Press, Sun-Bulletin digitized.

county_metadata.record_losses: No significant record losses. Courthouse records intact.
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created from Genesee County in 1808 (organized 1817). Southwestern NY with Cattaraugus and Allegany Indian Reservations (Seneca Nation). Olean was major oil boom town.
county_metadata.research_tips: Cattaraugus County Clerk in Little Valley holds deed records from 1817. Surrogate's Court has probate from same period. Pre-1808 records in Genesee County. Significant Seneca Nation presence with Cattaraugus and Allegany Reservations within county boundaries. Seneca Nation records maintained separately at Seneca-Iroquois National Museum in Salamanca. For oil boom era (1860s-1890s), Olean Public Library has petroleum industry records. Cattaraugus County Historical Museum in Machias has genealogical files. Early settlers from New England via Holland Land Company. Lumber industry records document forest workers.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Deed records from 1817; county clerk office

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed and mortgage records from organization.
Wills, administrations from organization.
Seneca Nation records on Cattaraugus and Allegany Reservations. Treaty records, annuity rolls.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Partial. Many records held by Seneca Nation with restricted access.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census from 1820 plus NY state census.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Complete federal. Some state years missing.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Birth and death from statewide registration.
Olean Times Herald, Salamanca Press, Little Valley Hub.

county_metadata.record_losses: No significant losses. Records well preserved at county clerk office.
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created from Onondaga County in 1799. Named for Cayuga Nation of Iroquois Confederacy. Auburn is county seat with famous state prison (1816). Harriet Tubman resided in Auburn. Finger Lakes region.
county_metadata.research_tips: Cayuga County Clerk in Auburn holds deed records from 1799. Surrogate's Court has probate from the same year. Pre-1799 records in Onondaga County (and earlier Herkimer). Auburn was home to Harriet Tubman from 1859 until her death in 1913 and the Harriet Tubman National Historical Park preserves her home and AME Zion Church records. The Cayuga County Historian's office maintains genealogical files and cemetery transcriptions for county towns. Cayuga Museum of History has Auburn prison records and local family manuscripts. The county's Finger Lakes location means strong agricultural records. For Cayuga Nation records check with Cayuga Nation tribal offices. The Seward House Museum (William H. Seward) has family and political correspondence useful for tracking local families.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Deed records from 1799; county clerk office in Auburn

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed and mortgage records from county formation.
Wills, administrations, guardianships from 1799.
Federal from 1800, NY state census additional detail.
Birth and death from statewide registration.

source_profiles.source_name: Presbyterian and Methodist Records — Cayuga Co
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Church records from denominations serving the Finger Lakes region. Auburn had many congregations.
Auburn Citizen, Auburn Daily Advertiser digitized.

county_metadata.record_losses: No significant record losses. Records intact from organization.
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created from Genesee County in 1808 (organized 1811). Westernmost county of NY along Lake Erie and PA border. Jamestown is largest city. Chautauqua Institution (1874) is internationally famous. Wine grape region.
county_metadata.research_tips: Chautauqua County Clerk in Mayville holds deed records from 1811. Surrogate's Court has probate from same period. Pre-1808 records in Genesee County. The county was settled primarily by New Englanders moving west via Lake Erie. The Chautauqua Institution archives (from 1874) document the famous educational assembly and its attendees from across the nation. Fenton History Center in Jamestown maintains extensive genealogical collections including family files, cemetery records, and city directories. For Swedish immigrant research, Jamestown had one of the largest Swedish populations outside Sweden and the Fenton Center has Swedish church records and immigration documents. The Patterson Library in Westfield has Holland Land Company records relating to early settlement grants.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed and mortgage records from county organization.
Wills, administrations from 1811.
Records of Swedish immigrant community in Jamestown area. Church records, naturalization, fraternal organizations.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival through Fenton History Center collections.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census from 1820 plus NY state census.
Birth and death from statewide registration.
Jamestown Journal, Fredonia Censor, Dunkirk Observer.

county_metadata.record_losses: Minor fire in 1862 damaged some early records but most survived. Overall good preservation.
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created from Tioga County in 1836. Elmira is county seat and was major Civil War POW camp site. Mark Twain had summer home here. Located at junction of Chemung and Cohocton Rivers.
county_metadata.research_tips: Chemung County Clerk in Elmira holds deed records from 1836. An 1862 fire damaged some early records but most land and probate records survived. Pre-1836 records in Tioga County. Elmira was a major Civil War prison camp (Elmira Prison, 1864-1865) and the Chemung County Historical Society has extensive POW records and cemetery records for Confederate soldiers buried at Woodlawn National Cemetery. Mark Twain's connection to Elmira (buried at Woodlawn) brings researchers to the area. The Steele Memorial Library has local history and genealogy collections. Elmira College archives have records of the first college to grant degrees to women equally with men (1855). For African American genealogy, Elmira's AME church records date from the 1840s.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Deed records from 1836; county clerk Elmira

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed and mortgage records from formation.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Mostly complete. Minor gaps from 1862 fire.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Records of Confederate POWs at Elmira Prison Camp. Burial records at Woodlawn National Cemetery.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good. Historical Society has compiled prisoner lists.

county_metadata.record_losses: No significant courthouse fires or record losses.
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created from Tioga and Herkimer Counties in 1798. Central NY hill country. Norwich is county seat. Agricultural region with hop-growing history. Chenango Canal (1834-1878) connected to Erie Canal.
county_metadata.research_tips: Chenango County Clerk in Norwich holds deed records from 1798 and Surrogate's Court has probate from the same year. For pre-1798 records check both Tioga and Herkimer Counties depending on the specific town location. The Chenango County Historical Society in Norwich maintains genealogical files, cemetery transcriptions, and published local histories. The Guernsey Memorial Library in Norwich has a local history room with county newspapers and directories. The county's hop-growing industry (1820s-1890s) generated unique agricultural records. Many original settlers came from Connecticut and Massachusetts and migration patterns track through New England town records. The Chenango Canal era (1834-1878) brought economic development and canal worker records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Deed records from 1798; county clerk Norwich
